title: Bruins trade for former first-round pick Lukas Reichel
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded a 2026 sixth-round pick to Vancouver for forward Lukas Reichel.
Lukas Reichel, 23, drafted 17th overall in 2020, has scored 22 goals in 188 NHL games.
Reichel will start with the Bruins' AHL affiliate in Providence, with potential NHL upside.

title: 2 Boston restaurants to pay  $400K for failure to pay service fees to staff, AGO alleges
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Carrie Nation Cocktail Club and The Dubliner must pay $422,093 for mislabeling and misallocating a service fee.
The fee, meant for service employees, was charged as a 'service fee' instead of 'hospitality fee' from May 2023 to June 2024.
East Coast Tavern Group claims a point-of-sale system error and has begun repaying the restitution and penalties.

title: Patriots reportedly had this trade offer for A.J. Brown rejected by Eagles
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots' offer of first and third-round picks for A.J. Brown was rejected by Eagles, according to Anthony Gargano.
Eagles seek a first-round pick plus a second-round pick to trade A.J. Brown, per NFL insider Mike Garafolo.
NFL insiders speculate A.J. Brown could be traded by the end of this weekend amid ongoing trade discussions.

title: Connecticut woman running 2026 Boston Marathon after Alzheimer’s strikes her family again
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colleen Graham, 33, from Norwalk, Connecticut, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon inspired by her father's Alzheimer's diagnosis.
Graham's participation honors her father, a lifelong Boston sports fan and former marathon runner, as he battles Alzheimer's.
She aims to raise $30,000 for Alzheimer's research, motivated by her family's personal experiences with the disease.

title: New England travel tips for the last days of winter
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Maine Maple Sunday weekend occurs March 21-22 with tours, tastings, and events across sugarhouses and farms statewide.
South Boston St. Patrick’s Day Parade on March 15 will reverse its route, expecting up to 1 million visitors.
Halfinger Farms in Higganum, Conn. reopens April 1 with over 150,000 daffodils available for picking.

title: Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A GoFundMe campaign supports Crystal Johnson-Clementi's family after her death from a Southborough car crash.
Johnson-Clementi, 37, died February 20; she survived breast cancer and worked at the Horseshoe Pub & Restaurant.
The fundraiser, started by her niece, has raised over $46,000, nearing its goal for the family's expenses.

title: Jayson Tatum: Active for the game, never questionable in the tunnel
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the court for the Boston Celtics after a 10-month absence.
Tatum's pre-game fashion choices are set to resume, drawing interest from fashion-conscious fans.
Celtics teammates showcased high-end street style while Tatum was sidelined, maintaining fan engagement.

title: How Susan Hurley’s legacy inspired her son to run the Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Max McGillivray is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his mother, Susan Hurley, supporting the Justice Health Initiative.
Susan Hurley founded CharityTeams, raising $42 million over 18 years for nonprofits and charity runners in the Boston Marathon.
McGillivray and his brother continue Hurley's cause, focusing on securing marathon bibs for Justice Health Initiative's healthcare efforts.

title: Back Bay’s ‘ugly duckling’ is getting a makeover
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Developers are converting an 11-unit building at 18 Commonwealth Ave into a luxury single-family home for $7.85 million.
The renovation includes a 5-floor, 8,100 square foot home with a two-car garage and private rooftop terrace.
Boston Modern Design is restoring the facade to its original design, with market listing expected around May for $20-24 million.

title: Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprises Boston bar crowd, picks up everyone’s tab
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Country star Bailey Zimmerman paid the bar tab at J.J. Foley's Cafe after a show at Boston's TD Garden.
Zimmerman surprised patrons by visiting the South End pub during his 'Different Night Same Rodeo Tour'.
J.J. Foley's Cafe owner praised Zimmerman and highlighted the pub's welcoming atmosphere for musicians.
